name: China
summary: Day 46 / Ceasefire Day 6. CNN reported US intel says China preparing to ship MANPADs to Iran [CLAIMED — China denied]. If confirmed, major escalation of Chinese material support from passive (radar, BeiDou) to active anti-air weapons. Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UN Hormuz resolution. China Hormuz oil imports collapsed from 5.35M to ~1.22M bpd — despite being in IRGC allies pricing tier. Three Chinese VLCCs still not confirmed through Hormuz. Structural advantage extraction continues but MANPAD claim introduces potential US-China confrontation vector. Shanghai Composite at ~3,986. USD/CNY at 6.83 (flat). PBOC not defending.

signals.content: Chinese MFA spokesperson: attack and killing of Khamenei is 'grave violation of sovereignty.' 'Firmly opposes and strongly condemns.'
China provided missile spare parts, advanced radar systems, BeiDou-3 encrypted navigation for Iranian military, satellite intelligence support. But no troops.
Al Jazeera opinion: 'China's silence on Iran reveals its true priorities' — economic interests (trade with US, stability) outweigh Iran alliance obligations.

China continues as crisis beneficiary: yuan settlement at Hormuz toll booth (IRGC-tolled passages in yuan), Shanghai +0.2% on Monday (resilient vs global selloff), reduced Western competition for resources. Samsung exploring potential helium deals with Russian firms — Russia-China axis provides alternative supply chains. PBOC steady — yuan barely moved (USD/CNY 6.91). (Sources: yfinance, USNI News, Korea Plus)

China is a NET BENEFICIARY of the crisis: (1) Manufacturing PMI 50.4 in March (beat 50.1 expected), returning to expansion after 2 months contraction. (2) Chinese ships approved for Hormuz transit — selective opening rewards China's neutrality. (3) Yuan-denominated IRGC toll system at Hormuz deepens yuan internationalization at dollar's expense. (4) Chinese refineries buying discounted Iranian crude via yuan settlement. (5) Shanghai Composite only -0.4% while KOSPI -3.4% and Nifty -2.1% — China's insulation is remarkable. (6) Samsung/SK hynix exploring Russian helium deals — China could facilitate. China's economic insulation from this crisis IS intelligence about the geopolitical alignment: Iran's selective blockade rewards neutrality. (Sources: NBS/PMI data, Al Jazeera, USNI News, yfinance)

China positioning as potential peace broker per CNN — Beijing moving from posturing to possible active mediation, which would be a major geopolitical shift. China continues as net crisis beneficiary: ships approved for Hormuz, yuan toll system deepens yuan internationalization, refineries buying discounted Iranian crude. PMI 50.4 held. Shanghai relatively insulated (-0.5% while KOSPI -4.5% and Nifty -2.5%). If China brokers US-Iran deal, it would be the diplomatic equivalent of the 2023 Beijing Agreement that normalized Saudi-Iran ties — a second major Middle East diplomatic coup. (Sources: CNN, Al Jazeera, NBS data)

China reportedly banned urea exports until August — removes alternative supply for India and tightens the global fertilizer crisis (CF Industries +24.1% monthly, urea at $693-750/mt). USD/CNY at 6.88 (~flat). Shanghai Composite at 3,880 (-1.0% 1D, -5.6% monthly). Yuan-denominated Hormuz toll system continues — deepening yuan internationalization at dollar's expense. China remains Iran's primary economic lifeline via toll payments and discounted crude purchases. No new diplomatic moves visible this cycle — the CNN-reported peace broker positioning has not advanced. OPEC+ approved 206K bpd but China's Hormuz transit exemption means the supply constraint matters less for Beijing than for anyone else.

China is net beneficiary of crisis (discounted Russian/Iranian crude). Shanghai flat April 6 (+0.0% 1D); no urgency to act. Yuan 6.88 — PBOC NOT defending (mild depreciation tolerated). China is one of the friendly flags in Iran's three-tier Hormuz system — Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker confirms vessels flagged to China continue to transit. China's silence + uninterrupted oil access = strategic posture of structural advantage extraction.

Day 43. THREE CHINESE VLCCs tested Hormuz transit — HALTED. Cospearl Lake (Cosco, ~2M barrels Iraqi crude), He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu sailed east at near-top speed April 9 morning, broadcasting Chinese ownership on AIS, before halting at strait entrance. None completed passage. China is Iran's closest economic partner — if IRGC blocks Chinese ships, no other nation will attempt transit. Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UNSC Hormuz resolution (April 7) — China cited 'failed to capture root causes in balanced manner.' China benefits from preferential yuan-denominated access through IRGC toll system. Samsung/SK Hynix signed 3-5yr helium LTAs partly to diversify from China-dependent supply chains. China pushing domestic ASML-certified ultra-pure helium. (Source: Bloomberg AIS, Rigzone, Taipei Times, PBS, Al Jazeera, TrendForce)

Day 44. THREE CHINESE VLCCs STILL HALTED AT HORMUZ — NO COMPLETED TRANSIT [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. The three vessels (Cospearl Lake/Cosco ~2M barrels Iraqi crude, He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu) that halted at the strait entrance on April 9 have NOT completed transit as of April 11. No subsequent AIS reporting confirms passage for any of the three. This is structurally significant because: (1) China is Iran's closest economic and political partner, (2) China sits in the IRGC 'allies' pricing tier at the lowest toll ($0.50-0.70/bbl), and (3) these are Cosco/Chinese-state vessels, not shadow-fleet. If IRGC is NOT expediting these vessels, the bilateral management of the toll system is much more discretionary than the tiered price chart suggests — even allies must negotiate individually. This contrasts with the Russian VLCC MT Arhimeda which successfully tested INBOUND passage April 9-10. The asymmetry is intelligence: Russia is getting its allies-tier treatment while China is not, possibly because (a) Iraqi crude on Cospearl Lake is Iraqi-flag cargo that disputes the toll, (b) IRGC is holding Chinese VLCCs as negotiating leverage with Beijing on helium/yuan internationalization, or (c) the publicity of the halt itself is the point. Shanghai Composite at 3,986 (+0.5% 1D) — insulated as usual. USD/CNY 6.83 flat. PBOC not defending. China continues structural advantage extraction from the crisis but the Hormuz transit asymmetry vs Russia is a new data point worth monitoring. (Sources: Bloomberg AIS, Windward AI, Rigzone, markets.md)

CNN reported US intel says China preparing to ship MANPADs to Iran [CLAIMED — China denied]. If confirmed, escalation from passive support (radar, BeiDou, satellites) to active anti-air weapons delivery. Russia and China vetoed Bahrain's UN Hormuz resolution. China Hormuz oil imports collapsed from 5.35M to ~1.22M bpd — despite allies-tier pricing. MANPAD claim introduces potential US-China confrontation vector if US interdicts shipment.

edges.context: Providing tech support (radar, BeiDou, satellites, missile parts) and diplomatic backing. But no military involvement.
Joint UNSC sessions. Coordinated condemnation. Both providing limited support to Iran.
China is major oil importer through Hormuz. Closure affects Chinese energy supply. But IRGC specifically excluded China from blockade.
